name: French Onion Soup Gratinée
instructions: 1. Melt the butter in a large pot over medium-high heat. Stir in the onions and salt. Cook, stirring frequently, until onions are caramelized, about 35 minutes. 2. Stir in the chicken broth, beef broth, red wine, and Worcestershire sauce. Bundle parsley, thyme, and 1 bay leaf with kitchen twine; add to pot. Simmer, stirring occasionally, for 20 minutes. Remove and discard herb bundle. 3. Reduce heat to low; stir in the balsamic vinegar, and season with the salt and pepper. Keep soup warm while you prepare the toast. 4. Set an oven rack about a few inches from the heat source and preheat the oven's broiler. Toast French bread under the broiler, turning once, until well toasted. 5. Arrange 2 oven-safe bowls on a baking sheet. Fill each bowl two-thirds full with hot soup. Top each bowl with a slice of toasted bread, Gruyère cheese, and shredded Asiago cheese. Sprinkle a pinch of paprika over each. 6. Broil until cheese is bubbly and golden brown, about 5 minutes. Serve hot and enjoy!
simpleInstructions: 1. Caramelize onions with the butter and salt. 2. Add broths, wine, Worcestershire, herbs; simmer. Remove herbs. 3. Stir in the vinegar, season. 4. Broil bread. 5. Assemble soup bowls with soup, bread, cheese, paprika. 6. Broil until browned.
